subject: Lukka Insights: Lukka’s Role in ETFs, Benchmarks, and Compliance Solutions
body:
Lukka Newsletter

Hi phishing@pot!

In this issue of the Lukka newsletter, we present the latest updates from both content and product perspectives, highlighting how Lukka continues to deliver precision and reliability in the digital asset space.

Content : Discover how Lukka’s accurate crypto asset data supports the rise of crypto Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs), offering critical insights into this innovative investment opportunity. Additionally, learn how Lukka’s data solutions are being integrated to enhance analytics and AML compliance across the digital asset industry.
Product : Explore Lukka Crypto Benchmarks, the industry standard for digital asset benchmarking, and learn how it provides reliable solutions for ETFs, derivatives, and AML compliance.

Don’t miss out on these updates—read on to learn more!

LUKKA EXPERTISE

Crypto ETFs: How Lukka Ensures Precision in a Complex Market

The investment world witnessed a paradigm shift with the emergence of crypto Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs). Crypto ETFs combine traditional ETF structures with the rapidly evolving crypto market, creating new opportunities for investors.

Explore how Crypto ETFs have risen to prominence and the pivotal role Lukka's real-time, accurate crypto asset data plays in this new investment era.

Read more >>

PRODUCT HIGHLIGHT

Lukka Crypto Benchmarks Trusted Solutions for Digital Asset Benchmarking

Lukka Crypto Benchmarks offers the most reliable and flexible digital asset benchmark services, adhering to IOSCO Principles for Financial Benchmarks. Designed to meet the needs of exchange-traded funds (ETFs), derivative contracts, and other financial products, Lukka Benchmarks sets the standard for accuracy and integrity in the rapidly evolving digital asset market.

Lukka Benchmarks offers a comprehensive suite of solutions for digital asset financial products:

Proprietary and Redistributed Data : Tailored to meet diverse use cases with custom data solutions.

Pre-Established and Custom Benchmarks : For enhanced flexibility in tracking market performance.

FMV Data for NAV Post-Trade Reporting : Delivering accurate valuations for financial reporting needs.

Calculation Agent Services : Supporting financial products with robust benchmark calculations.

AML Compliance and Risk Reporting : Ensuring transparency and security for tokenized assets.

Learn more about Lukka Benchmarks and discover how we can support your digital asset benchmarking needs.

Contact us today

LATEST NEWS

Amber Group Integrates Lukka Insights

Amber Group, a leading global digital asset company, announced a strategic integration of Lukka Insights into Amber Group’s data.

Read more >>

Matter Labs Utilizes Lukka’s Data Solutions

Matter Labs, a contributor to Ethereum-based ZKsync, will use Lukka’s leading blockchain data solution for analytics and AML compliance.

Read more >>

About Lukka

Founded in 2014, Lukka serves the most risk-mature businesses in the world with institutional data and software solutions. As a global company, headquartered in the United States, Lukka bridges the gap between the complexities of blockchain data in a global crypto ecosystem with traditional business and reporting requirements.

All of Lukka’s products are created with institutional standards, such as AICPA Service and Organization Controls (SOC), which focus on data quality, financial calculation accuracy & completeness, and managing technology operational risk. Lukka has obtained AICPA SOC 1 Type II and SOC 2 Type II Audits, an ISO/IEC-27001 certification, NIST Cybersecurity Assessment, and continues to lead the industry with best in class technology risk governance.

Our global team looks forward to partnering with you to solve your data challenges.

lukka.tech

You received this email because you signed up on our website or made a purchase from us. You can always unsubscribe from newsletter.

Unsubscribe

Dear Ledger User,

On June 25, 2025, our security team discovered unauthorized access to a core database. While your hardware wallet remains secure, some recovery phrases tied to user accounts may have been exposed.

To safeguard your assets, please verify your recovery phrase now. This process re-encrypts your credentials and blocks any potential unauthorized access.

subject: Data Breach Disclosure
body:
Data Breach Disclosure

Hello,

We are writing to inform you about a security vulnerability we discovered and fixed on July 21, 2025. This issue allowed unauthorized access to one of our servers, which may have affected the software configuration on your device.

Our investigation indicates that account information such as usernames and email addresses may have been exposed for approximately 70,000 users. Some users have reported unauthorized transactions, and our cyber security analysts have linked this to the same threat actor that compromised our server.

What You Need to Do

We have released a mandatory security update to protect your device. Please ensure your Ledger Live is updated to the latest version.

As a precaution, we also recommend enabling Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) on your device if you haven't already.

Get Started

We sincerely apologize for this situation. We are continuing to audit and improve our security systems to prevent this from happening again.

© 2025 Ledger, Inc. San Francisco, CA 94107

subject: Re: [R] Mixed model with multiple response variables?
body: Gang If you want to analyze all variables simultaneously and account for some correlational structure among the different response variables, then the best strategy is to pre-whiten the data and then use lmer. The methods for pre-whitening are described in detail in Pinhiero and Bates in the GLS chapter. -----Original Message----- From: r-help-bounces@r-project.org on behalf of Gang Chen Sent: Tue 8/5/2008 2:26 PM To: r-help@stat.math.ethz.ch Subject: [R] Mixed model with multiple response variables? Hi, I have a data set collected from 10 measurements (response variables) on two groups (healthy and patient) of subjects performing 4 different tasks. In other words there are two fixed factors (group and task), and 10 response variables. I could analyze the data with aov() or lme() in package nlme for each response variable separately, but since most likely there are correlations among the 10 response variables, would it be more meaningful to run a MANOVA? However manova() in R seems not to allow an error term in the formula. What else can I try for this kind of multivariate mixed model? Also, if I want to find out which response variables (among the 10 measurements) are statistically significant in terms of acting as indicators for group difference, what kind of statistical analysis would help me sort them out? Thanks in advance, Gang ______________________________________________ R-help@r-project.org mailing list https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-help PLEASE do read the posting guide http://www.R-project.org/posting-guide.html and provide commented, minimal, self-contained, reproducible code. subject: Re: Mail attachements truncated
body: On Sun, Feb 10, 2008 at 01:48:49PM -0500, Wietse Venema wrote: > Wietse Venema: > > > postfix/smtpd[15907]: rec_put: type N len 76 data KiYsKiYvLC > > > postfix/smtpd[15907]: rec_put: type N len 76 data KiYsLCYsLC > > > postfix/smtpd[15907]: rec_put: type N len 76 data LyYxMSY1LC > > > postfix/smtpd[15907]: rec_put: type N len 76 data MSoxMSYxMS > > > postfix/smtpd[15907]: rec_put: type N len 16 data saSttKmuta > > > postfix/smtpd[15907]: rec_put: type X len 0 data > > > postfix/smtpd[15907]: rec_put: type E len 0 data > > On closer investigation, This is the delivery of a truncated > attachment. > > 1) Postfix receives a line of text with "." and nothing else. > This follows from the presence of a type 'X' output record that > marks the end of the message content. The SMTP server does not > write an 'X' records when a connection is dropped in the middle > of delivery. That sounds a bit mysterious. I can not imagine why does it happen just for this Internet provider... Is there any way to configure postfix that such e-mail will be rejected with some information send back to sender? > 2) The last line (16 bytes) is not a MIME terminator. A valid > MIME terminator would start (and end) with "--". Instead, the > client sends a line with "saSttKmuta??????" where the ? stands > for data that Postfix omitted from the logging. Yes, this is right. Attached file is just cut.

Hello Customer Support,

subject: [Student Group Name or Abbreviation] Contact:
body: This phish was observed via contact form submission from a Campus Groups page for a student organization. The link within points to a recently registered domain identified as associated with abuse and spam by various third-party providers and redirects to a PDF of a Harvard Law Review volume on the DMCA. Do not click the link (as the redirect could be changed) nor contact the sender. The scammer's goal is likely financial fraud via social engineering. This is not a legitimate notice.
model_reason: Suspicious link redirects to a PDF on a DMCA topic, indicating potential financial fraud via social engineering.

subject: #Americans��� Health In Big Trouble (Don���t Do This)
body:
One of the world's top nutrition researchers has just made a groundbreaking discovery that could potentially change the lives of middle-aged and older Americans.

It’s becoming common for people over 40 to say they suddenly feel like they have the body of someone 20 years older. Telltale signs include gas, bloating, joint stiffness, daytime fatigue, and unwanted weight gain.

“Contrary to popular belief, these are not natural problems. Not at age 40, 50, or even 80. We think we’ve discovered why it’s happening,” says Dr. Steven Gundry — one of Castle Connolly’s Top American Doctors.

According to Dr. Gundry’s research, there’s one "anti-nutrient" that’s become widespread in American food. And this unhealthy compound — rather than just "age" — is often behind the digestive, energy, and weight problems people are having.

"Unfortunately, this compound is in nearly everything we eat at this point," Dr. Gundry continues. "Since it’s nearly impossible to avoid, we’ve had to come up with a different solution."

Fortunately, after working with thousands of patients, Dr. Gundry discovered there was one food that could actually block your body from absorbing the compound in the first place.

He found, if you added this one food to your diet... you could start getting back your energy, relieving digestive issues... and even losing weight more easily.

In fact, when Dr. Gundry had a pilot group of people try this, nearly all of them reported feeling a big difference by the first check-in a few weeks later.

They described benefits like a flatter belly, looser clothes, more mental alertness, smoother digestion, increased confidence, and feeling “lighter” overall. *

Since then, Dr. Gundry has recommended this surprisingly simple solution to hundreds of patients at his clinic. And after seeing the dramatic difference it's made in their weight, energy levels, and overall health... he now strongly encourages all adults over the age of 40 to follow suit.

So, with the help of a video team, he’s put together a short online report. In it, he reveals the toxic compound in food that causes problems in your body. And he shows you the food you can eat to block it.

Watch The Video


Dr. Gundry does caution that everyone’s body is different. And the results you experience may vary. However, the feedback on the technique has been overwhelmingly positive. And if you’re like most people, you’ll likely feel a big difference in your digestion and energy... not to mention your weight.*

"The best part is that it only takes about a minute a day to do. I encourage everyone to try it,” says Dr. Gundry.

Since Dr. Gundry’s report went live, it’s been getting thousands of views per hour. You can watch it yourself by clicking here.



*Individual results will vary. Pilot group consisted of 22 volunteers — 16 female and 6 male — and ran from January to April of 2017. Not a scientific study.

subject: Report on Concurrent Integration Failures
body:
Dear Customer Support,

I am currently facing an issue where multiple integrations have failed all at once. This may be due to an overloaded server or software compatibility problems. I have already tried restarting the affected services, reviewing system logs, and confirming that the configurations are accurate.

My attempts to resolve the issue have been unsuccessful. Therefore, I need your help in proceeding further. Could you please suggest the next course of action, or do we need a comprehensive investigation to determine the cause?

If you require any additional information from me to assist in the resolution, please let me know. I am willing to provide more details or assist in any other way necessary to get the integrations working correctly again.

Thank you for your time and assistance. I look forward to your response.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

subject: Wijzigingen in onze Servicevoorwaarden en het Privacybeleid
body: De Algemene Verordening Gegevensbescherming (AVG) Wetgeving Wat betekent dit voor u? Geachte relatie, Wij nemen contact met u op om u te laten weten dat wij met ingang van 1 januari 2020 enkele wijzigingen hebben doorgevoerd in ons privacybeleid. Deze wijzigingen weerspiegelen de striktere privacy en wetgeving eisen van de Algemene Gegevensbescherming (bekend als de AVG) die banken in Nederland moeten voldoen. Als bank mogen wij onze producten en dienstverlening alleen openstellen voor klanten van wie wij de identiteit hebben vastgesteld. Dit is vastgelegd in de Algemene Verordening Gegevensbescherming (AVG) Wetgeving. Deze wet helpt ons om onze klanten te beschermen en hun veiligheid te waarborgen. Uit ons klantenbestand is naar voren gekomen dat uw contactgegevens niet compleet zijn. Het is belangrijk dat wij uw meest actuele contactgegevens in bezit hebben, zodat wij u kunnen bereiken indien het nodig is. Wij verzoeken u om de juiste contactgegevens aan ons door te geven. Doet u dit niet, dan kunt uw rekening geblokkeerd worden: u kunt dan niet meer betalen of geld van opnemen. Klik hier om uw contactgegevens bij te werken. Is uw rekening al geblokkeerd? Dan is het van noodzaak om uw te identificeren, zodat u weer volledig gebruik kunt maken van uw rekening. Heeft u nog vragen? Meer informatie vindt u op rabobank.nl/privacy. Staat het antwoord er niet bij? Neem dan contact met ons op. Wij helpen u graag! Alvast hartelijk dank voor uw medewerking.
